summary: Scan vLLM + deps prefill hot path vs ours (27B), verify each gap, rank levers

confirmed.title: Full-buffer v_new memset every GDN layer — ~97% is immediately overwritten (FLA never memsets, it masks with boundary_check)
confirmed.vllm_does: FLA/Triton (the backend GB10 actually resolves to — qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py:186-211, family(100) gate false for sm_121) allocates v_new as exactly T rows with NO zero-init and NO padding (chunk_delta_h.py:355 `v_new = torch.empty_like(u)`), and NEVER memsets it. Partial-tail chunks are handled for free by masked Triton I/O: the store uses `boundary_check=(0,1)` (chunk_delta_h.py:200-204) so out-of-range tail rows are simply not written, and the consumer chunk_o loads v with `boundary_check=(0,1)` (chunk_o.py:133) returning 0 for OOB. Zero HBM spent zeroing.
confirmed.we_do: LaunchChunkedPrefill over-allocates v_new by kChunk rows (cuda_gdn.cu:1698 `t_pad=t_tot+kChunk`, alloc :1715) and, before DeltaH, `cudaMemsetAsync`s the ENTIRE buffer `t_pad*hv_n*dv*sizeof` to 0 (cuda_gdn.cu:1854). DeltaH V1 then writes every real token row (i<len guards at :1216/:1229), i.e. all t_tot rows, so the memset of those t_tot rows is dead — only the ~kChunk padding rows the WMMA tiles over-sweep past a partial chunk actually needed zeroing. For hv_n=32,dv=128,bf16 that memset is (T+64)*8 KiB per layer × 31 GDN layers (~0.5 GB/forward; the flagged ~30 GB aggregate), ~97% overwritten. It sits serially on DeltaH's critical path (v_new must be finite before V2's reduction reads it) once per layer.
confirmed.why_faster: The tail rows only need to be FINITE, not zero: in DeltaH V2 v_new is the col-major A operand reduced against Kd whose tail rows are already 0 (cuda_gdn.cu:1249/:1255,:1268), and in ChunkO v_new is the B operand reduced against As whose tail is already 0 (:1360,:1397) — but 0*NaN=NaN, so only garbage/NaN in the ~kChunk over-swept rows must be cleared. Zeroing just those rows instead of the whole buffer removes ~97% of the memset's HBM write traffic from a GPU-bound, serial-on-critical-path step.
confirmed.fix: Replace the full `cudaMemsetAsync(v_new,...)` at cuda_gdn.cu:1854 with a tiny device kernel gridded (n_seq, hv_n) that, per (n,hv), zeros only v_new rows [qsl[n]+seqlen_n, qsl[n]+ceil(seqlen_n/kChunk)*kChunk) × dv (the slack the last partial chunk over-reads; the last sequence's slack also covers the trailing pad, so no separate pad memset). All zero-writes are idempotent so any inter-block overlap on tiny sequences is harmless, and it must run before DeltaH (already the case for the memset). This zeros <= n_seq*kChunk rows vs t_pad rows — ~97% less write traffic for a long prefill. (Alternative: stage the per-chunk v_new tile into shared with i>=len→0 in DeltaH V2 / ChunkO like Kd/Qs already do, eliminating the pre-pass entirely.)
confirmed.verify_notes:
Diff REAL in both sources. FLA (pinned vLLM chunk_delta_h.py:355) `v_new = torch.empty_like(u)` — no zero-init, exactly T rows (no kChunk pad), stored with boundary_check=(0,1) (line 204) and consumed with boundary_check=(0,1) in chunk_o.py:133; masked Triton block-ptr I/O handles partial tails, NEVER memsets. Backend: GB10=sm_121 (major 12) → is_device_capability_family(100)="any 10.x" (interface.py:472) is False and is_device_capability(90) False → resolves to "triton"/FLA, so FLA is the honest baseline. Ours (cuda_gdn.cu): t_pad=t_tot+kChunk (1698), v_new alloc t_pad rows (1715), full-buffer cudaMemsetAsync(v_new,0,t_pad*hv_n*dv*sizeof) at 1854 serial before DeltaH; DeltaH V1 writes ALL real rows [0,t_tot) with i<len guards (1216/1229) → memset of those rows is dead; V2 reads v_new col-major from global (1268) over-reading slack, Kd tail zeroed (1249/1255); ChunkO reads v_new from global (1397), As tail zeroed (1360) — so tail rows only need to be FINITE (0*NaN=NaN), matching the code comment at 1852-1853. Tight packing verified (tok0=qsl[n]+it*kChunk, 1664-1669/1957) → t_tot/t_pad=2048/2112≈96.97% ≈ "97% overwritten" for single long prefill.

ON PREFILL HOT PATH: yes. Memset is in LaunchChunkedPrefill WMMA branch, once per GDN layer per prefill forward, size scales with T; bf16 27B (dk=dv=128) satisfies wmma=(dk%kWM==0&&dv%kNB==0) so it hits this branch; ~31 GDN layers → ~0.5 GB/forward aggregate matches claim.

GAIN SURVIVES: yes and NOT the neutral-without-cudagraph case — a memset is bandwidth-bound real GPU work, and the fix swaps one kernel launch (memset) for one launch (tiny slack-zeroing prepass), so launch count is neutral; the win is ~97% of ~0.5 GB/forward HBM write traffic reclaimed on a GPU-bound, serial-on-critical-path step. Fix correctness verified: tight-packing means each seq's over-read slack [qsl[n]+seqlen_n, qsl[n]+ceil(seqlen_n/kChunk)*kChunk) overlaps the NEXT seq's real rows (non-last) or the global pad (last); pre-zeroing exactly those rows before DeltaH preserves the finiteness invariant against the cross-block race (block n V2 reads block n+1 not-yet-written rows) while DeltaH V1 overwrites interior rows each block reads within its own __syncthreads pass — sound.

CAVEAT on magnitude: "3-5" is optimistic end-to-end. ~0.5 GB / ~273 GB/s GB10 unified ≈ ~1.8 ms/forward memset; removing 97% saves ~1.75 ms ≈ ~1-2% end-to-end prefill (~2-3% GDN-step-local). Direction/reality confirmed; realistic e2e is at/below the low end of the estimate. Effort low: single-file change with existing VT_GDN_* A/B + chunked-vs-sequential parity-golden infra; needs multi-seq/tiny-seq edge-case parity testing.

confirmed.title: DeltaH/ChunkO pin 1 block/SM by holding the full [Dv,Dk]=128x128 f32 state in shared and never split V across the grid; FLA keeps a [BV<=64,64] register state, splits V, and software-pipelines
confirmed.vllm_does: FLA chunk_delta_h holds the recurrent state in REGISTERS as [BV,64] tiles b_h1..b_h4 (chunk_delta_h.py:86-92, BV in {32,64}) — no large shared state — and grids (cdiv(V,BV), N*H) (chunk_delta_h.py:357-358), so V=128 gives 2 V-blocks per head and the state footprint is a fraction of ours, admitting many resident blocks. It autotunes num_warps in {2,4} and num_stages in {2,3,4} (chunk_delta_h.py:32-40) so Triton software-pipelines the global loads. chunk_o likewise grids (cdiv(V,BV), NT, B*H) and autotunes BK,BV,num_warps,num_stages (chunk_o.py:31-40,170-171).
confirmed.we_do: GdnChunkDeltaHWmmaKernel keeps the whole [Dv,Dk]=128x128 f32 state Hf resident in dynamic shared (cuda_gdn.cu:1147, 64 KiB) and grids only (n_seq, hv_n) (cuda_gdn.cu:1747) — one block owns ALL of V and loops nb over it. The ~96 KiB dynamic shared pins the kernel to 1 resident block/SM (own comment cuda_gdn.cu:1816-1834), and the team compensated by widening to 768 threads (50% occ). ChunkO is the same shape ((nt_tot,hv_n), :1746) with no V-split. There is no cp.async/pipelining of the global tile loads.
confirmed.why_faster: Splitting V into the grid (like FLA's cdiv(V,BV)) both doubles the block count and halves the per-block state so more than one block fits per SM, and register/pipelined state hides the global-load latency the shared-bound 1-block/SM path cannot. DeltaH (4.3%) + ChunkO (3.6%) are the two largest GDN-chunk buckets and are occupancy/latency bound, not FLOP bound.
confirmed.fix: Restructure GdnChunkDeltaHWmmaKernel (cuda_gdn.cu:1131) and GdnChunkOWmmaKernel (:1287) to grid (n_seq, hv_n, NV) with BV=dv/NV (e.g. NV=2, BV=64): each block owns V-rows [i_v*BV,(i_v+1)*BV), holding only a [BV,dk] state slice (~32 KiB, 2 blocks/SM), mirroring FLA's b_h tiling. Add cp.async double-buffering of the w/k/v_new global tiles to emulate Triton num_stages. Update the launch (cuda_gdn.cu:1862/:1875) grid and the hstate/state indexing to the V-slice.
confirmed.verify_notes:
DIFF IS REAL IN BOTH SOURCES — verified line-by-line.

vLLM/FLA (pinned /home/mudler/_git/vllm/.../fla/ops):
- chunk_delta_h.py:86-92 register state b_h1..b_h4 = [BV,64] tiles, BV in {32,64}; NO large shared state. Grid (cdiv(V,BV), N*H) at :357-358 (i_v=program_id(0) at :70) → V IS split across the grid. Autotune num_warps{2,4} x num_stages{2,3,4} x BV{32,64} at :32-40 → Triton software-pipelines global loads. All exactly as claimed.
- chunk_o.py:170-171 grid (cdiv(V,BV), NT, B*H); autotune BK,BV,num_warps,num_stages at :31-40. As claimed.

Ours (/home/mudler/_git/vllm.cpp/src/vt/cuda/cuda_gdn.cu):
- DeltaH :1147 float* Hf = [Dv,Dk] f32 state resident in dynamic shared; grid_seq=(n_seq,hv_n) :1747/:1862 (blockIdx x,y only, :1138); ONE block loops nb over ALL of dv :1184 → no V-split. delta_bytes :1859 = dv*dk*4 + pool (~64KiB+ state → ~96KiB, matches comment). Occupancy comment :1816-1834 confirms 1 block/SM, widened to 768 threads (50% occ).
- ChunkO :1294 blockIdx x,y only (grid_chunk=(nt_tot,hv_n) :1746/:1875), full-dv output, no V-split.
- grep confirms ZERO cp.async/memcpy_async/__pipeline in the file → no pipelining of global tile loads, as claimed.

ON PREFILL HOT PATH: YES. These are the GDN chunked-prefill WMMA kernels (gdn_prefill chunked path), run per prefill over all chunks. Not decode.

NOT a launch-overhead/eager artifact: this is an in-kernel GPU-compute/occupancy/latency restructuring (grid shape + shared footprint + pipelining), so it shows up in eager mode and does NOT require a prefill cudagraph. The "neutral without cudagraph" caveat does not apply — the gain survives our GPU-bound constraint.

MECHANISM CORRECTION (adversarial): the claim's stated reason — "halve per-block state → 2 blocks/SM → more resident warps hide latency" — is PARTLY WRONG. The team's own comment (:1828-1832) establishes the SM is REGISTER-bound at the tuned width: 80 regs/thread × 64K reg file caps total resident threads at ~819 (=768 warps-multiple), reached already by widening 256→768. Splitting V into 2 blocks would NOT add resident warps — 2 blocks would split the SAME ~768-thread register budget (2×384). So the shared-memory 1-block/SM pin is NOT the binding occupancy ceiling; the register file is, and warp-level latency hiding is already near-maxed.

WHERE THE REAL RESIDUAL GAIN IS: (a) DeltaH grid=(n_seq,hv_n) is SMALL and SEQUENTIAL (one block owns a head's whole recurrence). For prefill of few sequences, n_seq×hv_n can be << SM count → many idle SMs; V-split (×NV) multiplies block count and fills SMs — a real win but via GRID-FILL, not "more warps/SM". ChunkO grid=(nt_tot,hv_n) is already large (nt_tot=sum of chunks), so it benefits far less. (b) cp.async/num_stages pipelining is orthogonal and legitimate — ours has zero, FLA autotunes num_stages 2-4; hides global-load latency independent of occupancy. Corroboration the technique is portable: our OWN decode kernel :553-673 already mirrors FLA (grid (NK=1,NV,N*HV), register b_h[BV,BK], nv=cdiv(dv,bv)); the chunk kernels are just un-migrated.

ESTIMATE SKEPTICISM: DeltaH 4.3% + ChunkO 3.6% = 7.9% of step; est "2-4" implies cutting these ~25-50% on TOP of the -32%/-25% already extracted by widening. Credible for DeltaH IF its grid is genuinely SM-underutilized (verify n_seq×hv_n vs SM count for the 27B), much weaker for ChunkO. I'd trim the honest estimate toward the LOW end (~1.5-2.5% of step), sourced mainly from DeltaH grid-fill + cp.async, and would gate the work on first measuring DeltaH's grid occupancy (nsys) rather than assuming a shared-memory occupancy win that the register file already caps.

EFFORT high: reshape both WMMA kernels to (n_seq,hv_n,NV) with V-slice state indexing, rework hstate/state layout, add cp.async double-buffering (new infra), and re-tune occ per NV — non-trivial and correctness-sensitive (must preserve chunked==sequential goldens).

confirmed.title: K/V global loads are fully exposed — no cp.async double-buffered pipeline (FA-2's core speed source)
confirmed.vllm_does: FA-2 loads K/V with cp.async and software-pipelines them one n-block ahead: the next-block K copy is issued right AFTER the QK^T gemm (flash_attn/src/flash_fwd_kernel.h:334-338 and 396-401) and the current-block V copy is issued BEFORE the QK^T gemm (flash_fwd_kernel.h:308-317 and 383-384), each followed by cute::cp_async_fence(); cp_async_wait<0> only blocks when the data is actually consumed. Net: all gmem K/V loads overlap with tensor-core QK^T/PV compute. This is what vLLM dispatches to on GB10 (cuda.py:153-160 puts FLASH_ATTN first for major==12; fa_utils.py:153-161 resolves fa_version=2).
confirmed.we_do: PagedFlashWmmaGqaFlash2VecBMKernel stages K/V with StageKeyBf16 (src/vt/cuda/cuda_paged_attn.cu:1257-1269) which is an ordinary synchronous 128-bit copy (d4[i]=s4[i]) — grep confirms ZERO cp.async / __pipeline / memcpy_async in the file. The tile loop (cuda_paged_attn.cu:1571-1688) is fully serial: K-load then __syncthreads (1588) -> QK^T then __syncthreads (1610) -> softmax then __syncthreads (1646) -> V-load then __syncthreads (1662) -> PV then __syncthreads (1687). Every gmem K/V load sits on the critical path with nothing overlapping it.
confirmed.why_faster: FA-2 hides K/V DRAM latency behind the WMMA/MMA math; our kernel exposes it. Our own header (cuda_paged_attn.cu:1247-1252) already measured staging at ~63% of an earlier version. With 5 hard __syncthreads per K/V tile and no prefetch, the attn kernel is load-latency-bound, not compute-bound.
confirmed.fix: Either (a) land the task-#22 FA-2 wiring (it already has this pipeline), or (b) rewrite the BM kernel's tile loop (cuda_paged_attn.cu:1571-1688) to a 2-stage cp.async ring: prime stage-0 K before the loop, and inside iteration i issue __pipeline_memcpy_async (cp.async.cg.shared.global, 16B) for stage (i+1) K and for stage-i V immediately after the QK^T store, then __pipeline_wait_prior(1)/(0) only right before the consuming WMMA. Replace StageKeyBf16's d4[i]=s4[i] with cp.async intrinsics.
confirmed.est_gain_pct: prefill-attn is 3.9% of prefill kernel time; removing exposed-load stalls plausibly cuts this subsystem 25-40% -> ~1.0-1.5% end-to-end prefill
confirmed.verify_notes:
Core architectural diff CONFIRMED in both sources by reading every cited line. FA-2 (vendored flash_fwd_kernel.h): cp.async double-buffered K/V pipeline is exactly as described — V copy BEFORE QK^T gemm (308-317, 383-384) + cp_async_fence (317,384); next-block K copy AFTER QK^T gemm (334-338, 396-401) + cp_async_fence (338,401); cp_async_wait<0> gates only at consumption (305,332,381,394); copy() uses cp.async tiled copy gmem_tiled_copy_QKV. Ours: StageKeyBf16 (1257-1268) is synchronous 128-bit d4[i]=s4[i]; BMKernel tile loop (1571-1688) fully serial with exactly 5 in-loop __syncthreads (1588,1610,1646,1662,1687); grep shows ZERO cp.async/__pipeline/memcpy_async in the whole file. On prefill hot path: yes — BMKernel is the prefill attn kernel (LaunchPrefillWmmaGqaFlash2VecBM), runs per prefill step/layer; prefill is NOT cudagraph-captured (comment L14). Gain survives constraints: YES — this is intra-kernel DRAM-latency hiding via cp.async overlapping WMMA math, a real GPU-side win, NOT an eager launch-overhead saving, so no prefill cudagraph needed; the task's launch-overhead caveat does not apply.

ADVERSARIAL CAVEATS (why not a slam-dunk lever): (1) The 'load-latency-bound, not compute-bound' premise is an INFERENCE from '5 syncthreads + no prefetch', NOT a measured ncu/nsys memory-stall profile — if the kernel is actually WMMA-compute- or barrier-bound the cp.async win shrinks materially; treat the 25-40% subsystem cut as an unproven upper bound. (2) Magnitude is small: prefill-attn cited at 3.9% of prefill → ~1.0-1.5% end-to-end ceiling even in the optimistic case, against a high-effort/high-risk paged-gather cp.async ring rewrite. (3) The claim's '~63% of an earlier version' attribution to header 1247-1252 is slightly wrong — that header states staging cut ~5x and 27.6→8.8 ms/layer (3.1x), no literal 63%. (4) Dispatch premise (FLASH_ATTN first, fa_version=2) confirmed at cuda.py:153-160 / fa_utils.py:160-162 ONLY for non-MLA GQA 27B (consistent with task #22 'full-attn'); MLA 27B on major==12 routes to TRITON_MLA (cuda.py:130-134). (5) NOT verified: whether vLLM's precompiled FA-2 binary actually runs on sm_121 GB10 vs runtime-falling-back — dispatch lists FA-2 first but sm_120/121 build coverage was not confirmed; confirm before treating FA-2 as the literal A-B baseline.

RECOMMENDATION: real, correctly-described, hot-path, cudagraph-independent — but low-ROI as a standalone rewrite (high effort/risk for ≤~1.5%). Best captured for free by landing the already-in-progress task #22 FA-2 wiring (option a), which vendors this exact pipeline; do not hand-roll the cp.async ring (option b) until a profile confirms the kernel is memory-latency-bound.

confirmed.title: Wire the RIGHT FA-2 instantiation and don't chase FA-3/FA-4/flashinfer — FA-2 sm80 IS the ceiling on GB10
confirmed.vllm_does: On GB10 (device_capability.major==12) vLLM's dense prefill resolves to FA-2, not FA-3/FA-4: fa_utils.py:153-161 only gives FA3 for major==9 and FA4 for major==10, else fa_version=2; fa_utils.py:243-256 additionally rejects FA4 for head_size>128 (our 256) on Blackwell. flashinfer's own auto backend also returns 'fa2' for non-SM90 (flashinfer-ref/utils.py:509-518). The exact hdim256 kernel is run_mha_fwd_splitkv_dispatch<bf16,256,Is_causal> = kBlockM=64, kBlockN=64, 4 warps/128 threads, num_splits=1 for prefill (flash_fwd_launch_template.h:163-169), causal via n_masking_steps=ceil(kBlockM/kBlockN)+1 masked blocks then unmasked loop (flash_fwd_kernel.h:298-375).
confirmed.we_do: Our dispatch (cuda_paged_attn.cu:2286-2300) always routes prefill to the hand-WMMA kernels and launches 8 warps / 256 threads (cuda_paged_attn.cu:2130: block=32*kWmmaWarps, kWmmaWarps=8), not FA-2's 4 warps/128 threads. Task #22 is mid-wiring the vendored FA-2 (only flash_fwd_split_hdim256_bf16_{,causal}_sm80.cu are present).
confirmed.why_faster: This is a grounding guardrail: the closable gap is FA-2's cp.async pipeline (findings 1-2), NOT a fancier kernel family. Targeting FA-3 (SM90-only) or FA-4 (SM100-only, hdim256-unsupported on Blackwell) or a flashinfer trtllm/cudnn prefill would be wasted effort — none of them is what vLLM runs here. Confirm the wiring instantiates the splitkv dispatch with num_splits=1 and Is_causal, at 128 threads.
confirmed.fix: In the FA-2 wiring (worktree flash_attn/), instantiate only run_mha_fwd_splitkv_dispatch<cutlass::bfloat16_t,256,Is_causal> with params.num_splits=1 and pass params.scale_softmax_log2 = scale*M_LOG2E; in cuda_paged_attn.cu:2290-2300 gate the FA-2 path on (is_prefill && d==256 && bf16) and keep the WMMA kernel only as fallback. Do NOT add FA3/FA4/cudnn code paths for sm_121.
confirmed.est_gain_pct: correctness/grounding of the ~3.4% task-#22 wiring; prevents mis-targeting effort
confirmed.verify_notes: Grounding guardrail CONFIRMED on both sides. vLLM fa_utils.py:153-162 — GB10 is sm_121 (major==12), neither ==9 (FA3) nor ==10 (FA4), so step-1 else-branch sets fa_version=2; and 238-250 additionally rejects FA4 for head_size>128&!=192 on major>=10, so any Blackwell hdim256 path (incl. the SM100 FA4 pick) collapses to FA2. flashinfer utils.py:509-518 auto backend returns 'fa2' for non-sm90a (GB10 qualifies). FA-2 vendored kernel exactly matches: launch_template.h:163-169 run_mha_fwd_splitkv_dispatch<T,256,Is_causal> => kBlockM=64, kBlockN=64 (Headdim>128 branch), 4 warps/128 threads; kernel.h:298-375 causal via n_masking_steps=ceil_div(kBlockM,kBlockN)[+1 non-even] masked loop then unmasked loop, using params.scale_softmax_log2 (grounds the fix). Only the two flash_fwd_split_hdim256_bf16_{,causal}_sm80.cu instantiations are present, causal one = <cutlass::bfloat16_t,256,true>. Ours: kWmmaWarps=8 => block=256 threads (line 2130), prefill always routed to WMMA (2286-2320), NO FA-2 branch yet; task #22 in_progress. On prefill hot path: YES (LaunchPaged is_prefill compute kernel). Gain survives: the guardrail's real value (don't chase FA3=SM90-only / FA4=SM100+ & hdim256-rejected / flashinfer=auto-fa2 anyway — all wasted effort on GB10) is solidly grounded. The WMMA->FA-2 swap is a KERNEL-COMPUTE difference (128 vs 256 threads, cp.async pipeline, tiling), not a host launch-overhead saving, so the 'neutral without a prefill cudagraph' caveat does NOT apply — any win is on-GPU compute, which is what matters GPU-bound. Caveats (not refuting): (1) the ~3.4% is task #22's own UNMEASURED estimate; source alone does not prove FA-2 beats our already-heavily-tuned WMMA (vec staging + GQA reuse + BM tiling) — FA-2 is the correct A/B REFERENCE (matches vLLM prod), not a guaranteed speedup. (2) num_splits=1 isn't literally set at 163-169 (caller-side heuristic) but our own comment at 2188-2189 corroborates prefill runs num_splits=1. Verdict: the claim as a grounding/anti-mis-targeting guardrail is accurate; treat the 3.4% as a hypothesis to A/B, not a proven number.

confirmed.title: Softmax uses __expf with a separate scale multiply; FA-2 folds scale into log2 domain and uses exp2f
confirmed.vllm_does: FA-2 pre-computes softmax_scale_log2 = scale*log2(e) once and applies exp2f directly: exp2f(score*scale_log2 - max_scaled) (softmax.h:67-88, driven by softmax_rescale_o at flash_fwd_kernel.h:343-344). exp2f maps to a single MUFU.EX2 hardware instruction; the online-rescale correction also uses exp2f (softmax.h:157).
confirmed.we_do: The BM kernel multiplies each score by scale as a separate op (cuda_paged_attn.cu:1625: sval = Sh[..]*scale) and then calls __expf(sval - m_new) (1635) plus __expf(m_old - m_new) for the running correction (1634). __expf itself expands to a multiply-by-log2e + EX2, so we pay an extra FMA per score element vs FA-2's pre-folded scale.
confirmed.why_faster: Folding scale*log2(e) into the exponent argument (done once on the host / per-row) removes one multiply per score element in the BN*BM-element inner loop and lets the compiler emit a bare exp2f, shaving the softmax pass that runs every tile.
confirmed.fix: Precompute scale_log2 = args.scale * 1.4426950408889634f on the host, pass it in, and in the softmax block (cuda_paged_attn.cu:1622-1638) replace sval=Sh[..]*scale + __expf(sval-m_new) with s_raw=Sh[..] then exp2f(fmaf(s_raw,scale_log2,-m_new_log2)), keeping m_new in the log2 domain; likewise the corr term uses exp2f((m_old-m_new)). Numerically equivalent to the current path.
confirmed.est_gain_pct: ~2-4% of the 3.9% attn subsystem -> ~0.1% end-to-end prefill (small, but free once BN/pipeline land)
confirmed.verify_notes:
Diff is REAL and correctly described in both sources. FA-2 (vendored worktree fa2-prefill): softmax.h:88 exp2f(tensor*scale - max_scaled), :76 max_scaled=max*scale, :157 online-rescale exp2f((prev-cur)*softmax_scale_log2); flash_fwd_kernel.h:343-344 pass params.scale_softmax_log2; host precompute cuda_flash_attn_fa2.cu:269 `p.scale_softmax_log2 = args.scale * M_LOG2E` — scale*log2e folded once, single exp2f (MUFU.EX2) with an FMA(score,scale_log2,-max_scaled). Ours (live prefill path, cuda_paged_attn.cu): :1625 sval=Sh[..]*scale (separate mul), :1634/:1635 __expf(m_old-m_new)/__expf(sval-m_new); __expf expands to *log2e + EX2, so per element we pay ~an extra mul/FMA vs FA-2's pre-folded FMA. Enclosing kernel PagedFlashWmmaGqaFlash2VecBMKernel (line 1497); file header lines 3-24 confirm M2.4 prefill flash kernels, never cudagraph-captured. Same *scale+__expf pattern in ~9 prefill kernel variants (78,195,320,452,595,702,914,1134,1394,1625). ON HOT PATH: yes — inside the per-key-tile softmax inner loop, runs per prefill token.

WHY gain_survives_constraints=false: (1) NOT a launch-overhead saving — it is genuine in-kernel FP32 arithmetic, so the "neutral without prefill cudagraph" caveat does NOT apply; we are GPU-bound on real compute. HOWEVER (2) magnitude is tiny by the claim's own estimate (~0.1% e2e, within noise); the removed FMA overlaps with MUFU(EX2) latency and the dominant WMMA QK^T / P·V matmuls, so realized gain is likely below the arithmetic-count fraction. (3) REDUNDANT with in-progress work: task #22 wires vendored FA-2 into the bf16 full-attn prefill (~3.4%), which already implements exactly this (scale_softmax_log2 + exp2f). FA-2 is NOT on main yet (grep FLASH_NAMESPACE/scale_softmax_log2 in src = empty; lives only in fa2-prefill worktree). If FA-2 lands, this WMMA kernel family is replaced/deleted and the micro-opt is moot; if it doesn't, you realize a sub-noise ~0.1%. Additional effort caveat: to capture the subsystem-level effect you'd have to edit all ~9 softmax sites and move the stored running-max into the log2 domain (affecting corr, s_l/s_m running stats and final normalization + -inf masking), i.e. not a purely local edit and carries numerical-parity risk — FA-2 proves it is numerically fine but it must be validated per site. Net: technically accurate lever, but practically dominated/subsumed by the FA-2 replacement already underway.

confirmed.title: Fuse q-RMSNorm + k-RMSNorm + RoPE (+ gate split) into one kernel on the full-attn path
confirmed.vllm_does: vLLM's QKNormRoPEFusionPass rewrites the split -> q_rms_norm -> k_rms_norm -> rotary_embedding chain into a single `fused_qk_norm_rope` op (QkNormRopePattern docstring showing the exact unfused->fused rewrite at /home/mudler/_git/vllm/vllm/compilation/passes/fusion/qk_norm_rope_fusion.py:31-52; FUSED_QK_ROPE_OP qk_norm_rope_fusion.py:27; pass registered at pass_manager.py:195). One kernel does both per-head RMSNorms and the RoPE rotation with q/k resident in registers.
confirmed.we_do: FullAttnBlock runs FOUR separate kernels over q/k, each round-tripping through HBM: vt::AttnGateSplit (src/vt/cuda/cuda_glue.cu:78-105) then vt::RmsNorm over the q heads (qwen3_5.cpp:1473) then vt::RmsNorm over the k heads (qwen3_5.cpp:1480) then vt::RopeNeox (qwen3_5.cpp:1485; kernel cuda_ops.cu:274). Each reads and rewrites q [T,Hq*Dh] / k [T,Hkv*Dh].
confirmed.why_faster: Collapsing 4 launches into 1 removes 3 full q/k HBM round-trips (each ~2*T*(Hq+Hkv)*Dh*4 bytes at f32) and 3 kernel launches per full-attn layer. Applies to the 11 full-attn layers; contributes to the RmsNormRow (3.5%) + RopeNeox (1.7%) prefill buckets. confirmed.verify_notes:
Confirmed in both sources. OUR SIDE: paged prefill hot path FullAttnBlockPaged (src/vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.cpp:1578-1601) runs 4 separate kernels over q/k — vt::AttnGateSplit (cuda_glue.cu:78-111) -> vt::RmsNorm on q (:1586) -> vt::RmsNorm on k (:1593) -> vt::RopeNeox (:1601; kernel cuda_ops.cu:274), each reading+rewriting q[T,Hq*Dh]/k[T,Hkv*Dh] through HBM. VLLM SIDE: The claim cites QKNormRoPEFusionPass/fused_qk_norm_rope (qk_norm_rope_fusion.py:26-52; pass_manager.py:193-195; kernel csrc/libtorch_stable/fused_qknorm_rope_kernel.cu:709) — REAL, but that compilation pass matches the NON-gated split(qkv)->rms->rms->rope pattern (no gate). This 27B is a GATED (attn_output_gate) Qwen3.5 model, so the exact analog is a DIFFERENT, stronger vLLM fusion: the model-level Triton kernel fused_qk_rmsnorm_rope_gate (vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_qk_norm_rope.py), docstring: collapses 'split -> GemmaRMSNorm -> RoPE -> gate chunk' into one launch, 'Currently used by the Qwen3.5 attention path'; selected in qwen3_next.py:318-336. PREFILL HOT PATH: yes — runs per prefill token across the 11 full-attn layers of the 27B.

CONSTRAINTS (skeptical read): the saving has two parts. (a) 3 fewer q/k HBM round-trips: RMSNorm/RoPE over q/k are memory-bound elementwise ops, so eliminating intermediate writes/reads is a genuine GPU-bound bandwidth win that DOES survive. (b) 3 fewer kernel launches/layer: largely NEUTRAL in graphed prefill (launch overhead amortized over T tokens); this is a decode/eager win, not a prefill win. Magnitude is small: scoped to 11/42 layers and only fractions of the RmsNormRow (3.5%) + RopeNeox (1.7%) buckets, and the fused kernel still must read q/k once and write once. Realistic prefill landing is the LOW end of the 0.5-1% estimate (~0.3-0.5%); the upper 1% leans on launch-count reduction that graphed prefill mostly neutralizes. Gain survives as a real bandwidth saving but is optimistic at the top of the range. EFFORT medium: vLLM's reference Triton kernel (~120 lines) is directly portable to a co-located vt CUDA op next to RopeNeoxKernel; wire into both FullAttnBlock and FullAttnBlockPaged, handle the f32/bf16 kf dtype variants (:1591 bf16 k_norm path), and match numerics (round-trip through INPUT_DTYPE, positions[0], precomputed cos_sin). confirmed.title: vLLM does NOT cudagraph the large-prefill hot path — it runs the Inductor-compiled graph EAGERLY; a prefill cudagraph is the wrong lever (fusion is)
confirmed.vllm_does: Default v1 cudagraph_mode=FULL_AND_PIECEWISE captures FULL graph for decode and only a PIECEWISE graph for prefill/mixed (vllm/config/compilation.py:613-615). That piecewise prefill graph only exists for token-buckets up to max_cudagraph_capture_size = min(max_num_seqs*2, 512) → 512 (vllm/config/vllm.py:1722-1726). At runtime CudagraphDispatcher.dispatch() returns CUDAGraphMode.NONE whenever num_tokens > max_size (vllm/v1/cudagraph_dispatcher.py:274-281), so any real prefill (long prompt / chunk up to max_num_batched_tokens=8192) runs the Inductor piecewise-compiled `call()` EAGERLY with no graph replay. vLLM deliberately does not graph the large-prefill hot path.
confirmed.we_do: We run prefill/mixed fully eager through Qwen3_5Model::Forward*/ForwardDevice (src/vllm/v1/worker/gpu/runner.cpp:605-613), graphing only pure_decode (runner.cpp:532,596 → Qwen3_5DecodeGraph). This is exactly what vLLM ALSO does for large prefill (eager) — so on the prefill hot path we are NOT missing a cudagraph. The measured prefill 0.77x gap is GPU-side kernel/fusion efficiency, not launch-gap idle: parent already measured async/host levers as neutral in prefill, i.e. host cudaLaunch is fully hidden behind the large GEMMs, so a cudagraph (which only removes launch-gap GPU idle) has ~0 headroom to recover.
confirmed.why_faster: vLLM is faster in prefill because of Inductor EPILOGUE FUSION (add+rmsnorm+quant, silu+quant, rope) folded into the big fp4/fp8 GEMMs — fewer, larger kernels + less HBM traffic — NOT because it graphs prefill. The decode graph won 24% only because decode GPU work per step is tiny (M=1..64) so ~1.4k launches at ~5us serialize into GPU idle; in prefill each GEMM has M=num_tokens (hundreds/thousands), so the same launch count overlaps fully behind GPU compute and launch idle ≈ 0. A prefill cudagraph therefore cannot close the 0.77x gap.
confirmed.fix: Do NOT build a prefill/mixed cudagraph capture path in runner.cpp expecting a launch-overhead win — it would be ~neutral on the large-prefill hot path (parent's own async/host-neutral measurement is the proof). Spend the effort on the fusion levers instead: fuse RmsNormRow→ScaledFp4Quant into one kernel and silu→fp4-quant into the MLP GEMM epilogue (src/vt/cuda/cuda_matmul_nvfp4_cutlass.cu epilogue + the standalone RmsNormRow/ScaledFp4Quant kernels), and fuse RopeNeox/qk-norm into the attention QKV path — mirroring vLLM's fuse_norm_quant/fuse_act_quant Inductor passes (vllm/config/compilation.py:121-124). These attack the 43% fp4-GEMM + 3.5% RmsNorm + 3% ScaledFp4Quant + 1.7% Rope kernel time directly, which a graph cannot.
confirmed.est_gain_pct: ~0-1% (prefill cudagraph on the large-prefill hot path); the real gap lives in the fusion levers, not here
confirmed.verify_notes:
CONFIRMED in both sources. vLLM (pinned /home/mudler/_git/vllm): default v1 cudagraph_mode=FULL_AND_PIECEWISE captures a FULL graph for decode and only a PIECEWISE graph for prefill/mixed (compilation.py:613-615). That piecewise graph exists only for token buckets <= max_cudagraph_capture_size = min(max_num_seqs*decode_query_len*2, 512) further capped by max_num_batched_tokens (vllm.py:1722-1726), and CudagraphDispatcher.dispatch() returns CUDAGraphMode.NONE whenever num_tokens > max_size (cudagraph_dispatcher.py:278). So any large prefill (chunk >~512 tokens) runs the Inductor-piecewise-compiled call() EAGERLY with no graph replay. Fusion is delivered by compile-time Inductor passes fuse_norm_quant/fuse_act_quant (compilation.py:121-124), which apply in eager execution too. Ours (src/vllm/v1/worker/gpu/runner.cpp): pure_decode gate (532-533) routes only pure-decode fp4/CUDA num_reqs<=64 through the decode CUDA graph (575-576, 596); prefill/mixed run fully eager via Qwen3_5Model/DenseModel::ForwardDevice/Forward (585-613) — i.e. exactly what vLLM also does for large prefill. cuda_matmul_nvfp4_cutlass.cu epilogue currently carries only the alpha scale (fusion_args, line 214) — rmsnorm/silu are NOT folded in, confirming they run as standalone kernels (the real lever).

(1) Diff REAL in both sources: YES. (2) On the prefill hot path: YES — the eager prefill forward and the NONE-dispatch decision run per prefill step. (3) Gain survives constraints: YES, meaning the ~0-1% estimate for a prefill cudagraph holds — prefill is GPU-bound (GEMM M=num_tokens=hundreds/thousands), host launch overhead overlaps behind compute, and a cudagraph only removes launch-gap idle, so ~0 headroom; vLLM's own refusal to graph large prefill (relying on Inductor fusion instead) is independent corroboration. Recommendation to NOT build a prefill/mixed cudagraph capture path and instead pursue fusion (RmsNorm->ScaledFp4Quant, silu->fp4-quant GEMM epilogue, rope/qk-norm) is correct; that fusion work is high effort (already partly underway: task #11 done, #14/#15/#16/#21 pending).

Caveats (do not refute, only qualify): (a) claim says max_num_batched_tokens=8192 but vLLM default is 2048 (scheduler.py:42) — not load-bearing, any value >~512 means large prefill exceeds the graph cap. (b) Scope: for SHORT prefills (<=~512 total tokens) vLLM DOES use a piecewise graph our eager path forgoes; the claim is correctly scoped to the LARGE-prefill hot path where both run eager. (c) The 0.77x/24%-decode numbers are parent measurements not independently reverifiable here, but the mechanism they rest on is confirmed in source.

confirmed.title: RoPE recomputes pow/cos/sin in DOUBLE per element per head every forward; vLLM does LDG lookups from a precomputed cos_sin_cache
confirmed.vllm_does: vLLM builds cos_sin_cache=[max_pos, rot_dim] ONCE at init via _compute_cos_sin_cache (vllm/model_executor/layers/rotary_embedding/base.py:94-103: freqs=einsum(t,inv_freq); cache=cat(cos,sin)). At runtime rotary_embedding_kernel reads them with plain cached global loads and zero transcendentals: cos_f/sin_f = VLLM_LDG(cos_ptr+x_index) / VLLM_LDG(sin_ptr+x_index) (csrc/libtorch_stable/pos_encoding_kernels.cu:17-31, cache_ptr = cos_sin_cache + pos*rot_dim at :92-94).
confirmed.we_do: RopeNeoxKernel recomputes the angle from scratch for EVERY (token, head, pair): double freq=pow(base, -2.0*pair/rot); double angle=p*freq; float c=cos(angle); float sn=sin(angle) (src/vt/cuda/cuda_ops.cu:293-296). All three are f64 transcendentals, and because the loop is keyed on head (cuda_ops.cu:281-282) they are recomputed (hq+hk)x redundantly even though the angle only depends on (pos, pair). On sm_121a (GB10) f64 throughput is a small fraction of f32, so this kernel is transcendental-bound, not memory-bound.
confirmed.why_faster: A table lookup (one cached f32 load of cos and sin) replaces pow+cos+sin in f64 and removes the (hq+hk)x per-head recomputation, collapsing RopeNeox from a f64-transcendental-bound kernel to a trivially memory-bound gather. The 1.7% RopeNeox slice should drop to a few tenths of a percent.
confirmed.fix: Precompute a device cos_sin_cache once (mirror _compute_cos_sin_cache): either [max_model_len, rot_dim] at weight-load stored like a ResidentWeight, OR a tiny per-step kernel that fills [T, rot_dim] for the unique positions in the batch and is reused by all 11 full-attn layers. Change RopeNeoxKernel (src/vt/cuda/cuda_ops.cu:274-302) to take a const float* cos_sin_cache and index cache + pos[tok]*rot + pair for cos and + half + pair for sin, deleting the pow/cos/sin/double math. Thread cache pointer through LaunchRope/RopeNeoxKernelCuda (cuda_ops.cu:304-329) and RopeArgs.
confirmed.verify_notes:
VERDICT: CONFIRMED. The diff is real and exactly as described in BOTH sources.

vLLM side (verified):
- base.py:58-63 builds the cache ONCE at init: register_buffer("cos_sin_cache", _compute_cos_sin_cache(), persistent=False).
- base.py:94-103 _compute_cos_sin_cache: inv_freq -> freqs=einsum("i,j->ij", t, inv_freq); cos=freqs.cos(); sin=freqs.sin(); cache=cat((cos,sin),-1) => [max_pos, rot_dim]. Computed in torch.float, cast to dtype (bf16) once.
- pos_encoding_kernels.cu:92-93 cache_ptr = cos_sin_cache + pos*rot_dim; :18-19/23-24 cos_f/sin_f = VLLM_LDG(cos_ptr+x)/VLLM_LDG(sin_ptr+x). ZERO transcendentals in the kernel — pure cached global loads. Exactly the claim.

Our side (verified):
- cuda_ops.cu:293-296: per (token,head,pair): double freq=pow(base,-2.0*pair/rot); double angle=p*freq; float c=cos(angle); float sn=sin(angle). All three (pow/cos/sin) are f64 transcendentals. Comment at :270 confirms this is deliberate ("Angle math in double ... to match the CPU reference numerics").
- Index space n = t*(hq+hk)*half (LaunchRope:309) with head decoded at :281 but angle depends ONLY on (pos,pair) at :292-294 => the (hq+hk)x per-head redundant recompute is real.

(2) PREFILL hot path: CONFIRMED. RopeNeox is called per full-attn layer on both the reference-prefill path (qwen3_5.cpp:1485) and the production step path (:1601), applied to q and k for every token every layer. Runs per prefill token per layer.

(3) Gain survives constraints: YES. This is NOT an eager-launch-overhead saving — kernel launch count is unchanged. It is a direct reduction of GPU COMPUTE: replacing 3 f64 transcendentals per element (pow/cos/sin, done in software on sm_121a where f64 throughput is a tiny fraction of f32) with a couple of cached f32 loads. A kernel with 2 loads + 2 stores + 3 f64 transcendentals per element is unambiguously transcendental-bound, so cutting the transcendentals directly cuts GPU time and survives a GPU-bound regime without any prefill cudagraph. The per-step-fill variant reduces work from L_layers x T x (hq+hk) x half f64-transcendentals down to T x half f32-transcendentals computed once and reused by all layers — a large reduction. The 1.7% -> ~0.3% (net ~1.2-1.6%) estimate is directionally sound; I could not independently re-derive the 1.7% profile slice from source alone, but the magnitude is consistent.

CAVEATS for the fix (not refutations): (a) numeric parity — our kernel intentionally computes in f64 to be bit-exact with the CPU reference; vLLM's cache is f32-computed then cast to bf16. Adopting the cache moves us onto vLLM's numerics (arguably better parity vs vLLM) but WILL change goldens vs the current CPU f64 reference, so golden re-baselining is required. (b) Memory: a resident [max_model_len, rot_dim] bf16 cache is modest (~tens of MB at long context); the per-step [T,rot_dim] fill kernel avoids that and is the cleaner mirror of vLLM. Effort is medium: small kernel change + thread cache ptr through RopeArgs/LaunchRope + build cache (resident or per-step) + re-anchor numerics/goldens.

confirmed.title: Full-attn preamble runs 4 separate kernels (split + q-RMSNorm + k-RMSNorm + RoPE); vLLM fuses all four into one kernel
confirmed.vllm_does: Qwen3Next attn uses fused_qk_rmsnorm_rope_gate: ONE Triton kernel over grid (num_tokens, num_q_heads+num_kv_heads) that per (token,head) does RMSNorm (var over head_dim, rsqrt), partial NeoX RoPE via cos_sin_cache LDG, and the gate copy for q heads (vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_qk_norm_rope.py:17-114; dispatched at vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next.py:344-358). The q/gate/k activations are read once and the normalized+rotated q/k plus copied gate are written once.
confirmed.we_do: FullAttnBlockPaged issues four back-to-back launches, each doing a full global read+write of the q/k tensors: vt::AttnGateSplit (src/vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.cpp:1581), vt::RmsNorm q (:1586), vt::RmsNorm k (:1593), vt::RopeNeox (:1601). Across the 11 full-attn layers this is 4x the launch overhead and ~4 extra [T,Hq*Dh] global round-trips per layer on the memory-bound prefill preamble.
confirmed.why_faster: Fusing to one kernel keeps q in registers across split->norm->rope->gate, eliminating ~3 global round-trips of the [T,Hq*Dh] q buffer and 3 kernel launches per attn layer (x11 layers), and lets the RoPE half reuse the just-computed normalized value instead of reloading it.
confirmed.fix: Add a fused CUDA kernel modeled on the existing GdnPostConvKernel (src/vt/cuda/cuda_gdn.cu:314-371, which already fuses split+l2norm+gating over grid (T,H+1)): grid (T, Hq+Hkv), per block do the shared-mem RMSNorm reduction (reuse RmsNormRowKernel math), apply partial NeoX RoPE from the finding-1 cos_sin_cache, and copy the gate for q heads. Replace the four calls at qwen3_5.cpp:1581/1586/1593/1601 (and the non-paged twin at 1468/1473/1480/1485) with the single launch. This also lands tasks #14 and #15 together.
confirmed.verify_notes:
CONFIRMED in both sources. vLLM: fused_qk_norm_rope.py:17-201 is ONE Triton kernel, grid (n_tokens, num_q_heads+num_kv_heads) (line 171), per-(token,head) doing RMSNorm var/rsqrt (62-63) + partial NeoX RoPE from cos_sin_cache (75-107) + gate copy for q heads (109-114); q_gate/k read once, q/k/gate written once. It is the DEFAULT path, not dead code: use_fused_qk_norm_rope_gate = attn_output_gate AND is_neox_style AND is_cuda AND text_only (qwen3_next.py:318-323), all true for the gated-attn 27B on the GB10; dispatched at 344-358. Ours: four separate full-global-RW device ops confirmed at both paged FullAttnBlockPaged (AttnGateSplit 1581, RmsNorm q 1586, RmsNorm k 1593, RopeNeox 1601) and non-paged twin (1468/1473/1480/1485); AttnGateSplitKernel (cuda_glue.cu:78) is a plain global read-of-qgate/write-of-q+gate; intermediates qf/gatef/dqn are all DType::kF32 (2x the bytes of vLLM's bf16-register fusion), RopeNeox in-place.

HOT PATH: yes. FullAttnBlockPaged is the paged prefill path (dispatched 2259/2297), runs per full-attn layer (~10-11; code comment says x10 at line 1170) every prefill step; the four kernels run per token per layer.

CONSTRAINT (3) - survives, with nuance: the dominant saving is a REAL DRAM-traffic reduction, not launch overhead. Fusing keeps q in registers across split->norm->rope, eliminating ~2-2.5 f32 round-trips of [T,Hq*Dh] plus ~1 of [T,Hkv*Dh] per layer. That is genuinely bandwidth/GPU-bound in prefill and needs NO prefill cudagraph to pay off. The secondary launch-overhead component (~3 fewer launches x ~10 layers = ~30 launches) is LIKELY NEUTRAL because we're GPU-bound (kernels queue back-to-back on the stream). NOTE: the gate write is NOT saved (it is a required output written once in both paths), and a fused kernel must still write f32 q (attention consumes f32 query), so only the intermediate round-trips are eliminated, not the final outputs.

MAGNITUDE: 1.5-2.5% is plausible but assumption-sensitive. Rough estimate at Hq*Dh~4096, Hkv*Dh~512, T~2048, 10 layers, f32: ~1.4 GB extra traffic ~= ~5 ms at GB10 ~273 GB/s; whether that is 1.5-2.5% depends on total prefill time (dims/T/MoE-active). Should be measured, not assumed. Our f32 intermediates make our unfused penalty LARGER than vLLM's bf16 fusion would incur, which if anything strengthens the case.

EFFORT: medium. A clear template exists (GdnPostConvKernel cuda_gdn.cu:314-371 already fuses split+l2norm+gating over grid (T,H+1) with shared-mem tree reduction); mirror it as grid (T,Hq+Hkv) reusing the RmsNorm row math + partial NeoX RoPE + gate copy, replace the 4 calls at 1581/1586/1593/1601 and the non-paged twin 1468/1473/1480/1485. Lands tasks #14 and #15 together. Recommend implement + A/B nsys the full prefill step to confirm the actual percent before crediting the estimate.

confirmed.title: CausalConv1d is scalar and serial per (seq,channel) — each x read K times, no token parallelism; vLLM tiles [BLOCK_M,BLOCK_N] and reuses the window in registers
confirmed.vllm_does: _causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel loads a 2D [BLOCK_M tokens, BLOCK_N channels] x tile once (tl.load(x_ptrs,...) at vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/ops/causal_conv1d.py:205-215) and unrolls the KERNEL_WIDTH sliding-window columns col0..col3 (:158-192), so each x element is read once and reused across all K window positions. It parallelizes over token chunks (program_id chunk_offset, BLOCK_M tiles) as well as channels, giving many more resident blocks for a long prefill sequence.
confirmed.we_do: CausalConv1dFwdKernel is one thread per (sequence, channel) that walks the whole sequence serially: for each token t it re-loads the K-wide window scalar-wise Load(x,(begin+ti)*c_dim+c) (src/vt/cuda/cuda_gdn.cu:90-101), so every x element is loaded K times, and the K weights are reloaded every token. The grid is only (ceil(c_dim/kBlock), n_seq) (cuda_gdn.cu:122-124): no token-dimension parallelism, so a single long prefill sequence runs t_len iterations serially on ~c_dim threads with bf16 2-byte scalar loads (no 128-bit vectorization).
confirmed.why_faster: Loading each x element once (vs K times) cuts conv global traffic ~Kx, register-reusing the overlapping window raises ILP, 128-bit vector loads coalesce bf16 traffic, and tiling over token chunks fills the SMs for long prefill instead of serializing t_len tokens per thread.
confirmed.fix: Retile CausalConv1dFwdKernel (src/vt/cuda/cuda_gdn.cu:77-116): use a 2D block over (token-tile, channel-tile), load each channel's K-1 history + BLOCK_M x values once into registers/shared, unroll the K-tap dot with register reuse of the sliding window (like the col0..col3 unroll), vectorize the channel loads to 128-bit (float4/bf16x8) via the existing Load/Store helpers, and add token-chunk grid parallelism in LaunchConvFwd (cuda_gdn.cu:118-128). Landing this is task #23's 'parallel bf16 conv' lever.
confirmed.verify_notes:
Mechanism CONFIRMED on both sides. vLLM (causal_conv1d.py): grid=(num_program, cdiv(dim,BLOCK_N)) where num_program=sum(ceil(seqlen/BLOCK_M=8)) (lines 636-639, 689-693), so one long prefill seq -> ceil(T/8) token-chunk programs x ceil(dim/256) channel blocks = real token+channel parallelism; compute loop 411-465 loads each new token once (tl.load x_ptrs_1d) and register-reuses the window via col0=col1;col1=col2;col2=matrix_x shift (444-452), each x read once across K taps, BLOCK_N=256 vector loads. Ours (cuda_gdn.cu:77-128): one thread per (seq,channel) (81-82), serial for t in 0..t_len (90) with inner for j in 0..k re-loading the window scalar-wise Load(x,(begin+ti)*c_dim+c) (96) and reloading weights every token (100); grid=((c+256-1)/256, n) (123) has NO token dimension, so a single long prefill runs t_len iterations serially per thread. All accurately described.

PREFILL: called at qwen3_5.cpp:1098 in the GDN mixer over all T prefill tokens (dconv[T,conv_dim], qsl={0,T}) -> yes, per-prefill-token hot path, once per GDN layer.

Minor detail errors in the claim (not refuting): (a) the "2D [BLOCK_M,BLOCK_N] tile at :205-215" and "col0..col3 :158-192" citations point to the conv_state writeback / init-state load, not the actual compute-loop window reuse (:411-465, shift at :444-452) — but the mechanism they describe (each x once + register window reuse) genuinely exists there. (b) The 27B prefill site (qwen3_5.cpp:1081-1098) feeds f32 (mixed=MatmulF32D, dcw/dstate/dconv all f32) -> LaunchConvFwd<float,float>, so it is f32 4-byte scalar loads, NOT "bf16 2-byte" as stated. Immaterial to the mechanism (scalar, non-vectorized, K x redundant, no token parallelism hold for f32 too), and the vectorization/traffic argument still applies.

GAIN vs constraints: This is genuine GPU-bound waste, NOT an eager launch-overhead saving, so it survives without a prefill cudagraph. The dominant win is occupancy/serialization: our conv latency scales ~T (one thread walks all tokens) while vLLM caps per-program at BLOCK_M=8 iters with ceil(T/8) concurrent programs; the ~Kx global-traffic cut (Kw=4 for Qwen3-Next) is secondary. gain_survives_constraints=true. MAGNITUDE CAVEAT: the depthwise conv is a small fraction of GDN prefill (chunked delta-rule scan + projection GEMMs dominate), so 1.0-2.0x is credible as a per-conv-kernel speedup (likely >2x for very long single-request prefills due to the t_len serialization) but end-to-end prefill gain is diluted well below that. Fix = task #23's "parallel bf16 conv" lever: 2D (token-tile,channel-tile) block, load K-1 history + BLOCK_M x once into regs, unroll K-tap dot with sliding-window register reuse, vectorize channel loads, add token-chunk grid parallelism in LaunchConvFwd. Medium effort (focused kernel rewrite, not trivial, not multi-week).

confirmed.title: Activation fp4 quant emits LINEAR block-scales, then a separate SwizzleBlockscale kernel reorders them per GEMM every prefill; vLLM writes the swizzled scale directly inside the quant kernel (swizzle-in-quant)
confirmed.vllm_does: cvt_fp16_to_fp4 computes each fp8 block-scale's SWIZZLED tensor-core address inline and STG.8's it there in the same quant pass: nvfp4_quant_kernels.cu:84-91 calls cvt_quant_to_fp4_get_sf_out_offset (nvfp4_utils.cuh:168-200) which decomposes (row,kIdx) into the [numMTiles,numKTiles,32,4,4] atom layout (bit ops at :186-197) and returns the swizzled pointer; the write is cvt_warp_fp16_to_fp4 line 272 `*SFout=fp8SFVal`. is_sf_swizzled_layout defaults True (nvfp4_quant_kernels.cu:212). Result: ONE kernel produces already-swizzled SF; there is no separate swizzle op on vLLM's hot path.
confirmed.we_do: ScaledFp4QuantKernel writes LINEAR SF `scale[row*groups+g]=sc8` (cuda_matmul_nvfp4.cu:966), likewise the fused SiluMulFp4QuantKernel (:1051). Then MatmulNvfp4Fp4DirectD runs a SEPARATE SwizzleBlockscale kernel to reorder linear->atom layout for EVERY fp4 GEMM: qwen3_5.cpp:706-707 (`DBuf a_sf_sw...; vt::SwizzleBlockscale(...)`) -> SwizzleBlockscaleKernel cuda_matmul_nvfp4_cutlass.cu:269-299 (its dst_idx at :283 is the identical [Mp/128,32,4,Kp/4,4] mapping vLLM computes in-quant). ops.cpp:83 asserts out_scale is linear [M,K/16].
confirmed.why_faster: Eliminates one extra GPU kernel launch + a full read+write round-trip of the activation SF buffer per fp4 GEMM (~170 launches/prefill step across 42 layers x q/k/v/o/gate/up/down). On a GPU-bound prefill each tiny SwizzleBlockscale serializes on the same stream between quant and GEMM; folding the swizzle offset into the quant (which already visits every SF) makes it free.
confirmed.fix: In cuda_matmul_nvfp4.cu, replace the linear SF store at line 966 (ScaledFp4QuantKernel) and line 1051 (SiluMulFp4QuantKernel) with vLLM's swizzled offset: numKTiles=round_up(groups,4)/4; mTileIdx=row>>7; outerMIdx=row&31; innerMIdx=(row>>5)&3; kTileIdx=g>>2; innerKIdx=g&3; SFOffset=((int64_t)(mTileIdx*numKTiles+kTileIdx)<<9)|(outerMIdx<<4)|(innerMIdx<<2)|innerKIdx; scale[SFOffset]=sc8. Size out_scale as [Mp=round_up(m,128),Kp=round_up(groups,4)] and cudaMemsetAsync-zero it first (padding SFs must be 0, as SwizzleBlockscale did). Update the launchers ScaledFp4QuantKernelCuda:992 / SiluMulFp4QuantKernelCuda:1072 and the ops.cpp:83 shape check. In qwen3_5.cpp delete the a_sf_sw DBuf + SwizzleBlockscale at :706-707 and allocate qkv_as/gu_as/a_scale (lines 720,1434,2180) as the swizzled [Mp,Kp] buffer passed straight to MatmulNvfp4Cutlass. GATE this behind NvfpCutlassEnabled (default): the emulation GEMM MatmulNvfp4Fp4 reads linear SF (cuda_matmul_nvfp4.cu:1283 `a_scale[scaleRowA*groups+gc]`), so keep the linear store for that fallback path.
confirmed.verify_notes:
CONFIRMED in both sources. vLLM: scaled_fp4_quant defaults is_sf_swizzled_layout=True (_custom_ops.py:1495); cvt_fp16_to_fp4 computes each SF's swizzled tensor-core byte address inline via cvt_quant_to_fp4_get_sf_out_offset (nvfp4_utils.cuh:168-200, bit-ops :186-197) and STG.8's it there (write at nvfp4_utils.cuh:272 `if(SFout)*SFout=fp8SFVal`). ONE kernel emits already-swizzled SF — no separate swizzle op. Ours: ScaledFp4QuantKernel stores LINEAR scale[row*groups+g]=sc8 (cuda_matmul_nvfp4.cu:966), same for fused SiluMulFp4QuantKernel (:1051); then MatmulNvfp4Fp4DirectD runs a SEPARATE SwizzleBlockscale per GEMM (qwen3_5.cpp:706-707) whose kernel dst_idx (cuda_matmul_nvfp4_cutlass.cu:283) expands to the byte-identical atom-layout offset vLLM computes in-quant. ops.cpp:82-83 asserts linear [M,K/16]. All cited line numbers verified accurate; the proposed swizzle formula matches vLLM exactly.

HOT PATH: yes. NvfpCutlassEnabled() defaults ON (qwen3_5.cpp:676-682), so the per-GEMM swizzle is the DEFAULT 27B W4A4 path, run for q/k/v/o/gate/up/down every prefill step across all layers. Extra finding strengthening the claim: q/k/v share ONE qkv_as quant (line 1435) but each of the 3 MatmulNvfp4Fp4DirectD calls re-swizzles that same linear buffer (3x redundant); gate/up re-swizzle gu_as 2x. Swizzle-in-quant collapses these to one swizzle per shared quant — a slightly bigger reduction than the claim frames.

GAIN SURVIVES: yes, and the stated cudagraph caveat resolves in the fix's favor. Our cudagraph is DECODE-ONLY (Qwen3_5DenseDecodeGraph, runner.cpp:57-71); the 27B PREFILL is EAGER (ForwardDevice/Forward). So the hundreds of tiny per-GEMM swizzle launches pay full host launch overhead + a stream-serialized stall (GEMM depends on swizzled SF) + a real SF-buffer read+write round-trip, every prefill step — NOT neutralized, because there is no prefill cudagraph. It is not a pure launch-overhead mirage: the eliminated kernel is genuine dependent GPU memory work, and folding the swizzle offset into the quant (which already STG.8's the SF byte) is free bar a few int bit-ops. Modest magnitude (1-2%) is consistent; recommend an nsys/A-B to confirm since the SF buffer is small relative to the GEMM.

EFFORT medium: plumb a swizzled-output mode + padded [Mp=round_up(m,128), Kp=round_up(groups,4)] shape + cudaMemsetAsync-zero (padding SF must be 0) through ScaledFp4Quant/SiluMulFp4Quant kernels + their Cuda launchers + ops.cpp shape checks; gate on NvfpCutlassEnabled (note: ScaledFp4Quant currently only knows NativeFp4MmaEnabled via approx_recip, so a new flag/param must be threaded from the model layer down through the generic vt op); delete the a_sf_sw DBuf + SwizzleBlockscale at qwen3_5.cpp:707 and size qkv_as/gu_as/as as swizzled [Mp,Kp]; KEEP the linear store for the emulation fallback MatmulNvfp4Fp4 which reads linear a_scale at :1283. Caveat: the fused RmsNormResFp4Quant producer (referenced qwen3_5.cpp:692, task #11) also feeds the cutlass GEMM and likely stores linear too — it would need the same swizzled-store treatment for full coverage; the claim's fix omits it.

confirmed.title: SwizzleBlockscale re-run per projection on IDENTICAL shared activation scales (3x for fused qkv, 2x for gate/up) — redundant even keeping a separate swizzle kernel
confirmed.vllm_does: vLLM quantizes a merged projection's shared activation ONCE (MergedColumnParallelLinear / fused qkv), and because the quant already emits swizzled SF (nvfp4_quant_kernels.cu:84-91), the single swizzled SF buffer is reused by every fused output GEMM — the swizzle is never recomputed per projection.
confirmed.we_do: Under FuseQuantOnce the activation is quantized once (qwen3_5.cpp:1435 for qkv, :2181 for gate/up), but MatmulNvfp4Fp4DirectD is then called once PER projection (qwen3_5.cpp:1446/1452/1458 for q/k/v; :2187/2191 for gate/up) and each call allocates a fresh a_sf_sw and re-runs SwizzleBlockscale on the SAME qkv_as/gu_as input (qwen3_5.cpp:706-707). The swizzled output is bit-identical across the shared GEMMs, so 2 of 3 (qkv) and 1 of 2 (gate/up) swizzles are pure recomputation.
confirmed.why_faster: Hoisting the swizzle to once-per-shared-activation removes ~half of the SwizzleBlockscale launches + their SF read/write traffic on the prefill hot path. Lower-risk partial capture of the swizzle-in-quant win (kernel untouched); fully subsumed once finding #1 lands.
confirmed.fix: Swizzle the shared activation SF once at the fuse site: in qwen3_5.cpp after the fused ScaledFp4Quant (:1435, :2181) compute a single a_sf_sw and pass the already-swizzled SF into a new MatmulNvfp4Fp4DirectD overload that skips its internal SwizzleBlockscale (:707). i.e. move the SwizzleBlockscale out of MatmulNvfp4Fp4DirectD (:706-707) to the caller so q/k/v (and gate/up) share one swizzled buffer.
confirmed.verify_notes:
The DIFF is real in both sources (line numbers cited are ~correct; real file is src/vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.cpp).

OURS (default benchmark config, VT_NVFP4_CUTLASS unset => NvfpCutlassEnabled()==ON): MatmulNvfp4Fp4DirectD (694-715) allocates a fresh a_sf_sw and re-runs vt::SwizzleBlockscale (706-707) on EVERY call. Under FuseQuantOnce (default ON) the shared activation is quantized once (qkv: 1435; gate/up: 2181) but the GEMM is called once per projection on the SAME shared scale buffer (qkv_as at 1446/1452/1458 in FullAttnBlock and 1560/1566/1572 in FullAttnBlockPaged; gu_as at 2187/2191 in DenseMlpBlock). SwizzleBlockscale(707) takes ONLY a_scale as input and a_sf_sw dims (Mp=round_up(T,128), Kp=round_up(H/16,4)) depend only on M=T,K=H — identical across q/k/v (and gate/up) and independent of the weight N. So the swizzled output is bit-identical and 2-of-3 (qkv) + 1-of-2 (gate/up) swizzles are pure recomputation. Confirmed by reading the code.

VLLM: cutlass nvfp4 apply_weights (kernels/linear/nvfp4/cutlass.py:56-73) does ONE scaled_fp4_quant(is_sf_swizzled_layout=True) — the quant kernel writes the SF directly at the swizzled offset (nvfp4_quant_kernels.cu:84-91 cvt_quant_to_fp4_get_sf_out_offset; sf_m=round_up(numRows,128) at :55) with NO separate swizzle kernel — then ONE cutlass_scaled_fp4_mm over the MERGED qkv/gate_up weight, then slice_nvfp4_output splits q/k/v. Weight scale swizzled once at load (:37). So vLLM swizzles the activation SF exactly once per merged projection and never re-swizzles per output. Confirmed.

HOT PATH: yes. FullAttnBlock/FullAttnBlockPaged (qkv) and DenseMlpBlock (gate/up) run once per decoder layer per forward; prefill processes T>1 tokens, so the swizzles run per prefill step per layer.

GAIN DOES NOT SURVIVE as estimated (0.5-1 is optimistic). Caveats: (1) The redundancy exists ONLY in the cutlass path; the token-exact correctness gate pins VT_NVFP4_CUTLASS=0 (emulation MatmulNvfp4Fp4, no swizzle at all) — but the throughput/benchmark default is cutlass ON, which is the right denominator. (2) SwizzleBlockscale is a trivial kernel: it moves ~T*(H/16) bytes (fp8 SF) and writes ~Mp*Kp bytes (~a few hundred KB for T~512), i.e. sub-microsecond of actual GPU bandwidth per call, a negligible fraction of the 3 heavy fp4 GEMMs it feeds. Removing 3 redundant swizzle launches/layer mostly removes eager LAUNCH overhead + a tiny serial GPU bubble. Prefill is eager (no cudagraph), so the launch overhead IS exposed/capturable — but the path is GPU-bound (per project memory), so a launch that hides behind GEMM work yields little; realistic capture is well below 0.5%. (3) This is a strict SUBSET win: we still issue 3 separate GEMMs (weights not merged — that is task #21 "merged projection GEMMs"), so hoisting the swizzle does NOT reach vLLM parity and is fully subsumed by the merged-GEMM + swizzle-in-quant items (task #18 lists "swizzle-in-quant"; task #21 the merged GEMM).

Bottom line: the redundant-re-swizzle gap is REAL in both sources and on the prefill hot path (verdict CONFIRMED as a real diff), but it is a low-risk, low-reward launch-overhead/SF-traffic micro-opt on a GPU-bound eager path whose value is over-estimated and subsumed by the bigger merged-GEMM / swizzle-in-quant work. Proposed fix (hoist swizzle to the fuse site + a MatmulNvfp4Fp4DirectD overload that accepts a pre-swizzled SF and skips 706-707) is LOW effort and correct in principle; prefer folding it into the merged-GEMM change rather than shipping standalone.

confirmed.title: Full-attn q/k/gate/attn glue materializes f32 intermediates to HBM; vLLM fuses split+qk-RMSNorm+RoPE+gate into ONE bf16 kernel with no HBM round-trip
confirmed.vllm_does: Qwen3NextAttention calls fused_qk_rmsnorm_rope_gate (imported qwen3_next.py:25; called :344-357): a single kernel does the gated q split + QK-RMSNorm + partial NeoX RoPE + gate copy, consuming bf16 qkv and returning bf16 q, k and gate. The per-head normalized/roped q,k and the gate are never written to HBM as f32 intermediates; flash attention then takes the bf16 query against the bf16 KV cache.
confirmed.we_do: FullAttnBlockPaged runs this as 4 separate kernels that each spill f32 to HBM: AttnGateSplit writes qf f32 + gatef f32 [T,Hq,Dh] (src/vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.cpp:1579-1581); RmsNorm writes dqn f32 [T*Hq,Dh] and dkn f32 [T*Hkv,Dh] (:1584-1593); RopeNeox mutates dqn/dkn in place f32 (:1601); PagedAttention is handed the f32 query qn3 and writes dattn f32 (:1639,1650); SigmoidGateBf16 rereads dattn f32 + gatef f32 (:1653-1655). qf/gatef/dqn/dattn are 24KB/token each (Hq*Dh=6144) across the 16 full-attn layers.
confirmed.why_faster: Fusing to one bf16 kernel (mirroring fused_qk_rmsnorm_rope_gate) eliminates the qf/gatef/dqn/dkn f32 HBM materialize+reread entirely (>100KB/token/layer of avoided traffic) plus 3 kernel launches; even short of full fusion, emitting these bf16 halves the RmsNormRow (3.5%) and RopeNeox (1.7%) operand traffic and lets the PagedFlashWmmaGqa (3.9%) query load bf16 fragments directly instead of f32. This is the memory-traffic half of pending task #14.
confirmed.fix: Add a fused AttnQkNormRopeGate CUDA kernel (split [q|gate], per-head gemma-RMSNorm over Dh, partial NeoX RoPE on the T-row positions, gate passthrough) writing bf16 q, bf16 k, bf16 gate — replacing the AttnGateSplit/RmsNorm x2/RopeNeox sequence at qwen3_5.cpp:1579-1601. Interim (no new kernel): allocate qf/gatef/dqn/dkn as bf16 and run RopeNeox bf16-in-place, then hand PagedAttention a bf16 qn3 and allocate dattn bf16 (:1639) so SigmoidGateBf16 reads bf16. Keep RMSNorm's f32 variance accumulation (kernel already upcasts via Load()).
confirmed.verify_notes:
Both sources verified line-by-line. vLLM: fused_qk_norm_rope.py:16-201 is ONE Triton launch (grid (n_tokens, q+kv heads)) consuming bf16 q_gate/k, allocating bf16 outputs (156-162), doing RMSNorm(f32 accum)+partial NeoX RoPE+gate copy with NO f32 HBM intermediates; called qwen3_next.py:344-357, imported :25 — exactly as claimed. Ours: FullAttnBlockPaged (qwen3_5.cpp:1518) runs AttnGateSplit->qf/gatef f32 (1579-81), RmsNorm->dqn/dkn f32 (1584-93), RopeNeox in-place f32 (1601), PagedAttention fed f32 qn3 writing dattn f32 (1639,1650), SigmoidGateBf16 rereads dattn+gatef f32 (1653-55) — every cited line checks out. On 27B fp4, qgate is already bf16 (1558) yet AttnGateSplit upcasts to f32 (comment 1555), so the f32 spill is real waste.

Prefill hot path: CONFIRMED. FullAttnBlockPaged called from RunLayerPaged/RunDenseLayerPaged (2259,2297) for every full-attn layer over T tokens; runs per prefill token.

Gain survives GPU-bound constraint: YES. This is genuine HBM-traffic reduction on memory-bound elementwise/norm kernels (bf16 vs f32 operands halves bytes moved -> less GPU time even when GPU-bound); full fusion eliminates qf/gatef/dqn/dkn entirely. NOT a launch-overhead mirage — the ~3-launch saving is a smaller eager-mode bonus that would go neutral under a prefill cudagraph, but prefill runs eager here and the dominant component is memory traffic. Corroborated by memory note (closable gap = vLLM prefill fusion we run unfused) and pending task #14 verbatim, plus adjacent measured fusion wins (bf16 GEMM out +12.5% prefill). Est 2-3% is plausible/conservative given cited shares (RmsNorm 3.5% + Rope 1.7% + attention query-load fraction of 3.9% + AttnGateSplit + SigmoidGate), roughly halved.

Effort=medium and one caveat: the full fused CUDA kernel is new work (mirror the Triton kernel). The proposed INTERIM bf16-retype shortcut has a numerical-parity snag — our RmsNorm requires w.dtype==x.dtype (1588-92), so bf16 qf forces a bf16 q_norm weight, whereas vLLM keeps the norm weight in f32 (weight.float()+1.0, qwen3_next.py:347). Interim would deviate slightly from vLLM numerics unless RmsNorm is given an f32-weight/bf16-activation path; the full fused kernel avoids this by keeping f32 weight internally.

DeltaH/ChunkO pin 1 block/SM by holding the full [Dv,Dk]=128x128 f32 state in shared and never split V across the grid; FLA keeps a [BV<=64,64] register state, splits V, and software-pipelines

confirmed: 27B NVFP4 W4A4 prefill GEMM: we lifted vLLM's 2-config FALLBACK cutlass kernel; the graphed-vLLM oracle runs flashinfer mm_fp4 with a per-shape autotune over 8 SM120 tiles (32 configs)

Fuse q-RMSNorm + k-RMSNorm + RoPE (+ gate split) into one kernel on the full-attn path

confirmed: vLLM does NOT cudagraph the large-prefill hot path — it runs the Inductor-compiled graph EAGERLY; a prefill cudagraph is the wrong lever (fusion is)
refuted: If a prefill graph is ever built for the small/chunked-prefill regime, it MUST use dense token buckets — mirroring the decode graph's sparse {1,2,4,8,16,32,64} buckets would ADD GEMM compute via token-padding and regress
fp8 GEMM emits f32 for GDN in_proj_qkv/in_proj_z; vLLM's fp8 GEMM only ever emits bf16 (2x epilogue write + 2x downstream conv/gated-norm read)
Separate QuantFp8Static kernel instead of fusing the static fp8 quant into the preceding input_layernorm (vLLM fuses residual+rmsnorm+fp8-quant into one kernel)
Redundant per-projection fp8 activation quant: in_proj_qkv and in_proj_z each re-quantize the same normed activation (no quantize-once for the fp8 path)
Per-call cuBLASLt heuristic search + descriptor create/destroy on every fp8 GEMM (vLLM/PyTorch cache the plan)
Kernel-selection re-anchor: verify cuBLASLt nvjet still beats vLLM's cutlass sm120 fp8 at prefill M (prior A/B was decode-only M=64)

confirmed: RoPE recomputes pow/cos/sin in DOUBLE per element per head every forward; vLLM does LDG lookups from a precomputed cos_sin_cache
Full-attn preamble runs 4 separate kernels (split + q-RMSNorm + k-RMSNorm + RoPE); vLLM fuses all four into one kernel
CausalConv1d is scalar and serial per (seq,channel) — each x read K times, no token parallelism; vLLM tiles [BLOCK_M,BLOCK_N] and reuses the window in registers

Activation fp4 quant emits LINEAR block-scales, then a separate SwizzleBlockscale kernel reorders them per GEMM every prefill; vLLM writes the swizzled scale directly inside the quant kernel (swizzle-in-quant)
SwizzleBlockscale re-run per projection on IDENTICAL shared activation scales (3x for fused qkv, 2x for gate/up) — redundant even keeping a separate swizzle kernel
Silu-and-mul + NVFP4-quant fusion is DEFAULT-OFF in our dense MLP (42 layers) — vLLM fuses it by default
Full-attn QK-RMSNorm + partial-RoPE + gate-split done as 4-5 separate f32 kernels vs vLLM's ONE fused bf16 Triton kernel (11 layers)
RoPE recomputes sin/cos transcendentals per element every layer vs vLLM's precomputed cos_sin_cache load
refuted: GDN input projection runs 4 GEMMs (qkv, z, b, a) vs vLLM's 2 merged GEMMs (qkvz, ba); b/a are tiny-N GEMMs (31 layers)

confirmed: GDN in_proj mixed_qkv + causal-conv1d run in f32; vLLM keeps them bf16 (the two widest per-token tensors in the model)
GDN recurrence output (core_attn_out) + gated-RMSNorm inputs (z, norm weight) run in f32; vLLM keeps core_attn_out and z bf16
